main.c(3) Library Functions Manual main.c(3)

NAME

main.c - ascii-chat Client Main Entry Point

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct client_session_state_t
Client connection session state for session_client_like integration.

Functions


int client_main (void)
Client mode entry point for unified binary.

Variables


thread_pool_t * g_client_worker_pool = NULL
Global client worker thread pool. app_client_t * g_client = NULL
Global client application context. struct webrtc_peer_manager * g_peer_manager = NULL
Global WebRTC peer manager (legacy compatibility).

Detailed Description

ascii-chat Client Main Entry Point

This module serves as the main entry point for the ascii-chat client application. It orchestrates the entire client lifecycle including initialization, connection management, and the primary event loop that manages reconnection logic.

Architecture Overview

The client follows a modular threading architecture:

  • Main thread: Connection management and event coordination
  • Data reception thread: Handles incoming packets from server
  • Ping thread: Maintains connection keepalive
  • Webcam capture thread: Captures and transmits video frames
  • Audio capture thread: Captures and transmits audio data (optional)

Connection Management

The client implements robust reconnection logic with exponential backoff:

1.
Initial connection attempt
2.
On connection loss, attempt reconnection with increasing delays
3.
Maximum delay cap to prevent excessive wait times
4.
Clean thread lifecycle management across reconnections

Thread Lifecycle

Each connection cycle follows this pattern:

1.
Connection Establishment: Socket creation and server handshake
2.
Thread Spawning: Start all worker threads for the connection
3.
Active Monitoring: Monitor connection health and thread status
4.
Connection Loss Detection: Detect broken connections via thread exit
5.
Cleanup Phase: Join all threads and reset connection state
6.
Reconnection Cycle: Repeat from step 1 unless shutdown requested

Integration Points

  • server.c: Connection establishment and socket management
  • protocol.c: Initial capability negotiation and join packets
  • display.c: Terminal initialization and control
  • capture.c: Media capture subsystem initialization
  • audio.c: Audio subsystem initialization (if enabled)
  • keepalive.c: Ping thread management

Error Handling

The main loop implements graceful error recovery:

  • Fatal initialization errors cause immediate exit
  • Network errors trigger reconnection attempts
  • Signal handling for graceful shutdown (SIGINT, SIGWINCH)
  • Resource cleanup on all exit paths

Platform Compatibility

Uses platform abstraction layer for:

  • Socket operations and network initialization
  • Thread management and synchronization
  • Signal handling differences between Unix and Windows
  • Terminal I/O and control sequences

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

October 2025

Version

2.0

Definition in file client/main.c.

Function Documentation

int client_main (void )

Client mode entry point for unified binary. This function implements the complete client lifecycle including:

  • Client-specific subsystem initialization (display, capture, audio)
  • Server connection establishment with exponential backoff reconnection
  • Main event loop coordinating connection state and thread lifecycle
  • Media streaming (webcam frames and optional audio)
  • Frame display to terminal
  • Graceful shutdown and resource cleanup

Lifecycle Overview

The client follows this high-level state flow:

1.
Initialize: Set up display, capture, audio subsystems
2.
Connect Loop:
•
Establish TCP connection to server
•
Perform cryptographic handshake
•
Spawn worker threads (data reception, capture, keepalive, audio)
•
Monitor connection health
•
Detect connection loss via thread exit or socket errors
3.
Cleanup: Stop threads, close socket, reset terminal
4.
Reconnect: Exponential backoff, retry (unless fatal auth error)
5.
Exit: On user signal (SIGINT) or fatal error

Connection Failure Handling

Transient Errors (retryable):

  • Connection refused, timeout, DNS failure → exponential backoff retry
  • Network temporarily unavailable → wait and retry

Fatal Errors (no retry):

  • Authentication failed (CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED) → exit immediately
  • Host key verification failed (CONNECTION_ERROR_HOST_KEY_FAILED) → exit immediately

Thread Management

The client spawns several worker threads coordinated by the main thread:

  • Data Reception Thread: Receives and processes server packets
  • Webcam Capture Thread: Captures frames, transmits to server
  • Ping/Keepalive Thread: Sends periodic pings, detects timeouts
  • Audio Threads (optional): Captures/plays audio with Opus encoding

All threads check the global g_should_exit flag and exit cleanly on shutdown. Main thread waits for all threads to exit before reconnection or final cleanup.

Options

Options are already parsed by the main dispatcher (src/main.c) before this function is called. They are available via the global options API (GET_OPTION, etc.).

Key options affecting behavior:

  • opt_address: Server address or session string
  • opt_port: Server port (default 27224)
  • opt_audio_enabled: Enable audio capture/playback
  • opt_snapshot: Capture single frame and exit
  • opt_client_key, opt_server_key: SSH keys for authentication

Returns

Exit code:
  • 0: Success (clean exit or snapshot completed)
  • 1: Initialization/configuration error
  • 2: Connection fatal error (auth failed)
  • 130: Signal interrupt (SIGINT/Ctrl+C)

Example

# Invoked by dispatcher after options are parsed:
ascii-chat client --address localhost --audio
# Dispatcher (src/main.c) handles:
# - Option parsing
# - Log initialization
# - Signal handler setup
# Then calls client_main() which handles:
# - Subsystem init (display, capture, audio)
# - Connection loop with reconnection logic
# - Thread coordination
# - Graceful shutdown

See also

topic_client "Client Architecture Overview"

topic_client_main "Client Lifecycle Details"

topic_client_connection "Connection Management"

topic_client_protocol "Packet Processing"

Definition at line 622 of file client/main.c.

622                       {
623   log_debug("client_main() starting");
624 
625   // Register client crypto callbacks for TCP connection path
626   // The TCP path in lib/network/connection_attempt.c relies on these callbacks
627   // to handle cryptographic operations and key exchange
628   static const app_callbacks_t client_crypto_callbacks = {
629       .should_exit = should_exit,
630       .signal_exit = signal_exit,
631       .platform_get_monotonic_time_us = platform_get_monotonic_time_us,
632       .platform_sleep_ms = platform_sleep_ms,
633       .server_connection_set_ip = server_connection_set_ip,
634       .server_connection_set_port = server_connection_set_port,
635       .client_crypto_set_mode = client_crypto_set_mode,
636       .client_crypto_init = client_crypto_init,
637       .client_crypto_handshake = client_crypto_handshake,
638       .crypto_client_is_ready = crypto_client_is_ready,
639       .crypto_client_get_context = crypto_client_get_context,
640   };
641   app_callbacks_register(&client_crypto_callbacks);
642 
643   // Initialize client-specific systems (NOT shared with session_client_like)
644   // This includes: thread pool, display layer, app client context, server connection
645   int init_result = initialize_client_systems();
646   if (init_result != 0) {
647 #ifndef NDEBUG
648     // Debug builds: automatically fall back to test pattern if webcam is in use
649     if (init_result == ERROR_WEBCAM_IN_USE && !GET_OPTION(test_pattern)) {
650       log_warn("Webcam is in use - automatically falling back to test pattern mode (debug build only)");
651 
652       // Enable test pattern mode via RCU update
653       asciichat_error_t update_result = options_set_bool("test_pattern", true);
654       if (update_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
655         log_error("Failed to update options for test pattern fallback");
656         FATAL(init_result, "%s", asciichat_error_string(init_result));
657       }
658 
659       // Retry initialization with test pattern enabled
660       init_result = initialize_client_systems();
661       if (init_result != 0) {
662         log_error("Failed to initialize even with test pattern fallback");
663         webcam_print_init_error_help(init_result);
664         FATAL(init_result, "%s", asciichat_error_string(init_result));
665       }
666       log_debug("Successfully initialized with test pattern fallback");
667 
668       // Clear the error state since we successfully recovered
669       CLEAR_ERRNO();
670     } else
671 #endif
672     {
673       // Release builds or other errors: print help and exit
674       if (init_result == ERROR_WEBCAM || init_result == ERROR_WEBCAM_IN_USE || init_result == ERROR_WEBCAM_PERMISSION) {
675         webcam_print_init_error_help(init_result);
676         FATAL(init_result, "%s", asciichat_error_string(init_result));
677       }
678       // For other errors, just exit with the error code
679       return init_result;
680     }
681   }
682 
683   // Register cleanup function for graceful shutdown
684   (void)atexit(shutdown_client);
685 
686   // Register client interrupt callback (socket shutdown on SIGTERM/Ctrl+C)
687   // Global signal handlers (SIGTERM, SIGPIPE, Ctrl+C) are set up in setup_signal_handlers() in src/main.c
688   set_interrupt_callback(server_connection_shutdown);
689 
690 #ifndef _WIN32
691   // Register SIGWINCH for terminal resize handling (client-specific, not in framework)
692   platform_signal(SIGWINCH, client_handle_sigwinch);
693 #endif
694 
695   /* ========================================================================
696    * Client-Specific: LAN/Session Discovery
697    * ========================================================================
698    * This phase discovers the server to connect to via:
699    * - LAN discovery (mDNS)
700    * - Session string lookup (ACDS)
701    * - Direct address/port
702    */
703 
704   // LAN Discovery: If --scan flag is set, discover servers on local network
705   const options_t *opts = options_get();
706   if (opts && opts->lan_discovery &&
707       (opts->address[0] == '\0' || is_localhost_ipv4(opts->address) || strcmp(opts->address, "localhost") == 0)) {
708     log_debug("LAN discovery: --scan flag set, querying for available servers");
709 
710     ui_mdns_config_t lan_config;
711     memset(&lan_config, 0, sizeof(lan_config));
712     lan_config.timeout_ms = 2 * MS_PER_SEC_INT; // Wait up to 2 seconds for responses
713     lan_config.max_servers = 20;                // Support up to 20 servers on LAN
714     lan_config.quiet = true;                    // Quiet during discovery, TUI will show status
715 
716     int discovered_count = 0;
717     ui_mdns_server_t *discovered_servers = ui_mdns_query(&lan_config, &discovered_count);
718 
719     // Use TUI for server selection
720     int selected_index = ui_mdns_select(discovered_servers, discovered_count);
721 
722     if (selected_index < 0) {
723       // User cancelled or no servers found
724       if (discovered_count == 0) {
725         // No servers found - log message and prevent any further output
726         // Lock the terminal so other threads can't write and our error
727         // will be the last message
728         log_lock_terminal();
729 
730         // Log single message with embedded newlines to prevent multiple log entries
731         log_error("No ascii-chat servers found on the local network.\nUse 'ascii-chat client <address>' to connect "
732                   "manually.");
733 
734         // Exit without cleanup
735         platform_force_exit(1);
736       }
737       // User cancelled (had servers to choose from but pressed cancel)
738       log_debug("LAN discovery: User cancelled server selection");
739       if (discovered_servers) {
740         ui_mdns_free_results(discovered_servers);
741       }
742       return 1; // User cancelled
743     }
744 
745     // Update options with discovered server's address and port
746     ui_mdns_server_t *selected = &discovered_servers[selected_index];
747     const char *selected_address = ui_mdns_get_best_address(selected);
748 
749     // We need to modify options, but they're immutable via RCU
750     // Create a new options copy with updated address/port
751     options_t *opts_new = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(options_t), options_t *);
752     if (opts_new) {
753       memcpy(opts_new, opts, sizeof(options_t));
754       SAFE_STRNCPY(opts_new->address, selected_address, sizeof(opts_new->address));
755 
756       opts_new->port = (int)selected->port;
757 
758       log_debug("LAN discovery: Selected server '%s' at %s:%d", selected->name, opts_new->address, opts_new->port);
759 
760       // Note: In a real scenario, we'd update the global options via RCU
761       // For now, we'll use the updated values directly for connection
762       // This is a known limitation - proper RCU update requires more infrastructure
763     }
764 
765     ui_mdns_free_results(discovered_servers);
766   }
767 
768   // =========================================================================
769   // Client-Specific: Server Address Resolution
770   // =========================================================================
771   // Client mode supports:
772   // 1. Direct address/port (--address HOST --port PORT) - handled by options
773   // 2. LAN discovery (--scan) - handled above
774   // 3. WebSocket URL (direct ws:// or wss:// connection string)
775   //
776   // Note: Session string discovery via ACDS is handled by discovery mode only.
777   //       Client mode does NOT use ACDS or session strings.
778 
779   const char *discovered_address = NULL;
780   int discovered_port = 0;
781 
782   // Check if user provided a WebSocket URL as the server address
783   const options_t *opts_websocket = options_get();
784   const char *provided_address = opts_websocket && opts_websocket->address[0] != '\0' ? opts_websocket->address : NULL;
785 
786   if (provided_address && url_is_websocket(provided_address)) {
787     // Direct WebSocket connection
788     log_debug("Client: Direct WebSocket URL: %s", provided_address);
789     discovered_address = provided_address;
790     discovered_port = 0; // Port is embedded in the URL
791   }
792 
793   if (discovered_port > 0) {
794     log_debug("Client: discovered_address=%s, discovered_port=%d", discovered_address ? discovered_address : "NULL",
795               discovered_port);
796   } else {
797     log_debug("Client: discovered_address=%s", discovered_address ? discovered_address : "NULL");
798   }
799 
800   // Store discovered address/port in session state for client_run() callback
801   static char address_storage[BUFFER_SIZE_SMALL];
802   if (discovered_address) {
803     SAFE_STRNCPY(address_storage, discovered_address, sizeof(address_storage));
804     g_client_session.discovered_address = address_storage;
805     g_client_session.discovered_port = discovered_port;
806   } else {
807     const options_t *opts_fallback = options_get();
808     if (opts_fallback && opts_fallback->address[0] != '\0') {
809       SAFE_STRNCPY(address_storage, opts_fallback->address, sizeof(address_storage));
810       g_client_session.discovered_address = address_storage;
811       g_client_session.discovered_port = opts_fallback->port;
812     } else {
813       g_client_session.discovered_address = "localhost";
814       g_client_session.discovered_port = 27224;
815     }
816   }
817 
818   // Initialize connection context for first attempt
819   asciichat_error_t ctx_init_result = connection_context_init(&g_client_session.connection_ctx);
820   if (ctx_init_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
821     log_error("Failed to initialize connection context");
822     return 1;
823   }
824 
825   /* ========================================================================
826    * Configure and Run Shared Client-Like Session Framework
827    * ========================================================================
828    * session_client_like_run() handles all shared initialization:
829    * - Terminal output management (force stderr if piped)
830    * - Keepawake system (platform sleep prevention)
831    * - Splash screen lifecycle
832    * - Media source selection (webcam, file, URL, test pattern)
833    * - FPS probing for media files
834    * - Audio initialization and lifecycle
835    * - Display context creation
836    * - Proper cleanup ordering (critical for PortAudio)
837    *
838    * Client mode provides:
839    * - client_run() callback: connection loop, protocol startup, monitoring
840    * - client_should_reconnect() callback: reconnection policy
841    * - Reconnection configuration: attempts, delay, callbacks
842    */
843 
844   // Get reconnect attempts setting (-1 = unlimited, 0 = no retry, >0 = retry N times)
845   int reconnect_attempts = GET_OPTION(reconnect_attempts);
846 
847   // Configure session_client_like with client-specific settings
848   session_client_like_config_t config = {
849       .run_fn = client_run,
850       .run_user_data = NULL,
851       .kind = SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_CLIENT,
852       .discovery = NULL,
853       .custom_should_exit = NULL,
854       .exit_user_data = NULL,
855       .keyboard_handler = NULL, // Client mode: server drives display
856       .max_reconnect_attempts = reconnect_attempts,
857       .should_reconnect_callback = client_should_reconnect,
858       .reconnect_user_data = NULL,
859       .reconnect_delay_ms = 1000,         // 1 second delay between reconnection attempts
860       .print_newline_on_tty_exit = false, // Server/client manages cursor
861   };
862 
863   log_debug("[CLIENT_MAIN] About to call session_client_like_run() with %d attempts", reconnect_attempts);
864   asciichat_error_t session_result = session_client_like_run(&config);
865   log_debug("[CLIENT_MAIN] session_client_like_run() returned %d", session_result);
866 
867   // Network client lifecycle is owned by connection_context_cleanup() after the session returns.
868 
869   // Cleanup connection context
870   log_debug("[CLIENT_MAIN] About to call connection_context_cleanup()");
871   connection_context_cleanup(&g_client_session.connection_ctx);
872   log_debug("[CLIENT_MAIN] connection_context_cleanup() returned");
873 
874   // Cleanup session log buffer (used by splash screen in session_client_like)
875   // NOTE: session_log_buffer_destroy() is already called in session_client_like_run()
876   // during its cleanup phase, so we don't call it here to avoid double-destroy of mutex
877 
878   log_debug("[CLIENT_MAIN] ascii-chat client shutting down");
879 
880   // IMPORTANT: Stop worker threads and join them BEFORE memory report
881   // atexit(shutdown_client) won't run if interrupted by SIGTERM, so call explicitly
882   log_debug("[CLIENT_MAIN] About to call shutdown_client()");
883   fflush(stderr);
884   shutdown_client();
885   log_debug("[CLIENT_MAIN] shutdown_client() returned");
886   fflush(stderr);
887 
888   // Cleanup remaining shared subsystems (buffer pool, platform, etc.)
889   log_debug("[CLIENT_MAIN] About to call asciichat_shared_destroy()");
890   asciichat_shared_destroy();
891 
892   // Log final session statistics before exit
893   // Note: Frame count is logged in protocol_stop_connection() if connection was established
894   // This is a fallback in case the connection never started
895   return (session_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) ? 0 : 1;
896 }




References options_state::address,
    app_callbacks_register(), ASCIICHAT_OK,
    asciichat_shared_destroy(), BUFFER_SIZE_SMALL,
    CLEAR_ERRNO, client_crypto_handshake(),
    client_crypto_init(), client_crypto_set_mode(),
    connection_context_cleanup(), connection_context_init(),
    crypto_client_get_context(), crypto_client_is_ready(),
    ERROR_WEBCAM, ERROR_WEBCAM_IN_USE,
    ERROR_WEBCAM_PERMISSION, FATAL, GET_OPTION,
    is_localhost_ipv4(), options_state::lan_discovery,
    log_debug, log_error, log_lock_terminal(),
    log_warn, ui_mdns_config_t::max_servers,
    MS_PER_SEC_INT, ui_mdns_server_t::name, options_get(),
    options_set_bool(), platform_force_exit(),
    platform_get_monotonic_time_us(), platform_signal(),
    platform_sleep_ms(), options_state::port,
    ui_mdns_server_t::port, ui_mdns_config_t::quiet,
    SAFE_MALLOC, SAFE_STRNCPY, server_connection_set_ip(),
    server_connection_set_port(), server_connection_shutdown(),
    SESSION_CLIENT_LIKE_KIND_CLIENT, session_client_like_run(),
    set_interrupt_callback(), should_exit(), signal_exit(),
    ui_mdns_config_t::timeout_ms, ui_mdns_free_results(),
    ui_mdns_get_best_address(), ui_mdns_query(),
    ui_mdns_select(), url_is_websocket(), and
    webcam_print_init_error_help().








Variable
  Documentation




app_client_t* g_client = NULL


Global client application context. Global application client
    context




Central connection and application state for the client. Contains
    transport-agnostic state: audio, threads, display, crypto. Network-specific
    state (socket, connection flags) is in the active transport client.




Initialized by app_client_create() in client_main().
    Destroyed by app_client_destroy() at cleanup.


Definition at line 152 of file client/main.c.






thread_pool_t* g_client_worker_pool = NULL


Global client worker thread pool. Global client worker thread
  pool




Manages all client worker threads including:


    
  
  • Data reception thread (protocol.c)
    • 
  
  • Webcam capture thread (capture.c)
    • 
  
  • Ping/keepalive thread (keepalive.c)
    • 
  
  • Audio capture thread (audio.c)
    • 
  
  • Audio sender thread (audio.c)
    •

Referenced by audio_sender_init(),
    audio_start_thread(), capture_start_thread(),
    keepalive_start_thread(), protocol_start_connection(), and
    protocol_stop_connection().






struct
  webrtc_peer_manager* g_peer_manager = NULL


Global WebRTC peer manager (legacy compatibility). Global WebRTC
    peer manager for P2P connections.




Client mode no longer uses WebRTC, but protocol.c still references
    this. Always NULL in client mode.


Definition at line 160 of file client/main.c.
